title: Positive and negative intervals
description: Identify intervals on the domain of a function where it's entirely positive or entirely negative.
topic_path: Intervals where a function is positive, negative, increasing, or decreasing

hints:
The function $f$ is positive $($i.e. $f(x)>0)$ whenever its graph is above the $x$-axis.

This is because the positive $y$-values are above the $x$-axis, and $y=f(x)$.



[[☃ image 1]]
Out of our options, the only correct interval is $-2<x<-1$.

options.alt: A coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by one, and the y-axis scales by sixteen. The graph of the function y equals f of x is below the x-axis from x equals negative five to negative four. It is above the x-axis from x equals negative four to negative one. It is below the x-axis from x equals negative one to one. It is above the x-axis from x equals one to four. The function is increasing from left to right unitl x equals negative three. The function decreases from x equals negative three to x equals zero. The function increases indefinitely after x equals zero.

hints:
The function $g$ is positive $($i.e. $g(x)>0)$ whenever its graph is above the $x$-axis.

This is because the positive $y$-values are above the $x$-axis, and $y=g(x)$.



[[☃ image 1]]
Out of our options, the only correct interval is $0<x<1$.

options.alt: A coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by one, and the y-axis scales by ten. The graph of the function y equals g of x is below the x-axis from x equals negative five to negative four and one-half. It is above the x-axis from x equals negative four and one-half to negative two and one-quarter. It is below the x-axis from x equals negative two and one-quarter to zero. It is above the x-axis from x equals zero to three and one-half. The function is increasing from left to right until x equals negative three and one-half. The function decreases from x equals negative three and one-half to x equals negative one. The function increases from x equals negative one to x equals two and one-half. The function decreases indefinitely after x equals two and one-half. All values are estimates.

hints:
The function $h$ is positive $($i.e. $h(x)>0)$ whenever its graph is above the $x$-axis.

This is because the positive $y$-values are above the $x$-axis, and $y=h(x)$.



[[☃ image 1]]
Out of our options, the only correct interval is $-1<x<0$.

options.alt: A coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by one, and the y-axis scales by ten. The graph of the function y equals h of x is above the x-axis from x equals negative five to x equals zero. It is below the x-axis from x equals zero to x equals three and one-half. It is above the x-axis after x equals three and one-half.  The function is decreasing from left to right until x equals two and one-half. The function increases indefinitely after x equals two and one-half. All values are estimates.

hints:

This is because the positive $y$-values are above the $x$-axis, and $y=f(x)$.



[[☃ image 1]]
Out of our options, the only correct interval is $1<x<2$.

options.alt: A coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by one, and the y-axis scales by one-half. The graph of the function y equals f of x is above the x-axis from x equals negative five to x equals negative three. It is below the x-axis from x equals negative three to x equals zero. It is above the x-axis from x equals zero to x equals three. It is below the x-axis from x equals three to five. The function is decreasing from x equals negative five to x equals negative one and one-half. The function increases from x equals negative one and one-half to x equals one and one-half. The function increases from x equals one and one-half to x equals five. All values are estimates.

hints:
The function $g$ is positive $($i.e. $g(x)>0)$ whenever its graph is above the $x$-axis.

This is because the positive $y$-values are above the $x$-axis, and $y=g(x)$.



[[☃ image 1]]
Out of our options, the only correct interval is $-2<x<-1$.

options.alt: A coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by one, and the y-axis scales by one-half. The graph of the function y equals g of x is below the x-axis from x equals negative five to x equals negative three. It is above the x-axis from x equals negative three to x equals three. It is below the x-axis from x equals three to x equals five. The function is increasing from x equals negative five to x equals negative one and one-half. The function decreases from x equals negative one and one-half to x equals zero. The function increases from x equals zero to x equals one and one-half. It decreases after x equals one and one-half. All values are estimates.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Select the interval where $h$ is positive.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The function $h$ is positive $($i.e. $h(x)>0)$ whenever its graph is above the $x$-axis.

This is because the positive $y$-values are above the $x$-axis, and $y=h(x)$.



[[☃ image 1]]
Out of our options, the only correct interval is $2<x<3$.

options.alt: A coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by one, and the y-axis scales by ten. The graph of the function y equals h of x is above the x-axis from x equals negative five to x equals negative four. It is below the x-axis from x equals negative four to x equals zero. It is above the x-axis from x equals zero to x equals four. It is below the x-axis from x equals four to x equals five. The function is decreasing from x equals negative five to x equals negative two and one-half. The function increases from x equals negative two and one-half to x equals two and one-half. The function decreases from x equals two and one-half to x equals five. All values are estimates.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Select the interval where $f$ is positive.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The function $f$ is positive $($i.e. $f(x)>0)$ whenever its graph is above the $x$-axis.

This is because the positive $y$-values are above the $x$-axis, and $y=f(x)$.



[[☃ image 1]]
Out of our options, the only correct interval is $-4<x<-3$.

options.alt: A coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by one, and the y-axis scales by ten. The graph of the function y equals f of x is below the x-axis from x equals negative five to x equals negative four and one-half. It is above the x-axis from x equals negative four and one-half to x equals negative two and one-quarter. It is below the x-axis from x equals negative two and one-quarter to x equals zero. It is above the x-axis from x equals zero to x equals three and one-half. It is below the x-axis after x equals three and one-half. The function is increasing from x equals negative five to x equals negative three and one-half. The function is decreasing from x equals negative three and one-half to x equals negative one. The function increases from x equals negative one to x equals two and one-half. The function decreases indefinitely after x equals two and one-half. All values are estimates.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Select the interval where $g$ is positive.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:

This is because the positive $y$-values are above the $x$-axis, and $y=g(x)$.



[[☃ image 1]]
Out of our options, the only correct interval is $1<x<2$.

options.alt: A coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by one, and the y-axis scales by four. The graph of the function y equals g of x is above the x-axis from x equals negative five to x equals two and one-half. It is below the x-axis after x equals two and one-half. The function is increasing from x equals negative five to x equals negative three. The function is decreasing after x equals negative three.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Select the interval where $f$ is positive.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:

This is because the positive $y$-values are above the $x$-axis, and $y=f(x)$.



[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by one, and the y-axis scales by one. The graph of the function y equals f of x is below the x-axis from x equals negative five to x equals negative one and one-half. It is above the x-axis from x equals negative one and one-half to x equals one and one-half. It is below the x-axis from x equals one and one-half to x equals five. The function is decreasing from x equals negative five to x equals negative three. The function is increasing from x equals negative three to x equals zero. The function decreases from x equals zero to x equals three. The function increases from x equals three to x equals five. All values are estimates.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Select the interval where $g$ is negative.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The function $g$ is negative $($i.e. $g(x)<0)$ whenever its graph is below the $x$-axis.

This is because the negative $y$-values are below the $x$-axis, and $y=g(x)$.



[[☃ image 1]]
Out of our options, the only correct interval is $2<x<3$.

options.alt: A coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by one, and the y-axis scales by one. The graph of the function y equals g of x is below the x-axis from x equals negative five to x equals three. It is above the x-axis after x equals three. The function is increasing from x equals negative five to x equals negative one. The function is decreasing from x equals negative one to x equals one. The function increases from x equals one to x equals five. All values are estimates.

hints:

It is above the x-axis from x equals two and one-half to x equals four. It is below the x-axis after x equals four. The function increases from x equals negative five to x equals negative three and one-quarter. The function decreases from x equals negative three and one-quarter to x equals negative one and one-half. The function increases from x equals negative one and one-half to x equals zero. It is decreasing from x equals zero to x equals one and one-half. It is increasing from x equals one and one-half to x equals three and one-quarter. It is decreasing from x equals three and one-quarters to x equals five. All values are estimates.
